title: 第 144 節
en: We sacrifice unto Ashi Vanguhi, the bright, high, strong, tallrformed, and merciful ; we sacrifice unto the Glory made by Mazda ; we sacrifice unto the Weal made by Mazda. We sacrifice unto P4re«di, of the light chariot; we sacrifice Unto the Glory of the Arj^as, made by Mazda ; we sacrifice stRdZAH U. 19 unto the awful kingly Glory, made by Mazda; we sacrifice unto that awful Glory, that cannot be forcibly seized, made by Mazda; we sacrifice unto the Glory of Zarathartra, made by Mazda.
26. A^tid.
We sacrifice unto Arjt&Z, who makes the world grow ; we sacrifice unto Mount Ushi-darena, made by Mazda, a God of holy happiness.
27. Asmin.
We sacrifice unto the shining Heavens; we sacrifice unto the bright, all-happy, blissful abode of the holy ones.
28. Zemydd.

We sacrifice unto the Earth, a beneficent God; we sacrifice unto these places, unto these fields ; we sacrifice unto Mount Ushi-darena, made by Mazda, a God of holy happmess; we sacrifice unto all the mountains, that are seats of holy happiness, of full happiness, made by Mazda, the holy and masters of holiness ; we sacrifice unto the awful kingly Glory, made by Mazda ; we sacrifice unto the awful Glory that cannot be forcibly seized, made by Mazda.
29. Mahraspand.

We sacrifice unto the MSLthra Spe^ta, of high glory; we sacrifice unto the Law opposed to the Da6vas ; we sacrifice unto the Law of Zarathurtra ;. we sacrifice unto the long-traditional teaching ; we sacrifice unto the good Law of the worshippers of Mazda ; we sacrifice unto the Devotion to the Mftthra Spe»ta ; we sacrifice unto the understanding that keeps the Law of the worshippers of Mazda ; we sacrifice unto c 2 2Q YASTS AND stR6zAHS.
the knowledge of the MJLthra Spe«ta; we sacrifice unto the heavenly "Wisdom, made by Mazda; we sacrifice unto the Wisdom acquired through the ear and made by Mazda.

30. An^rdn, We sacrifice unto the eternal and sovereign luminous space; we sacrifice unto the bright Gar6nmdna; we sacrifice unto the sovereign place of eternal Weal ; we sacrifice unto the jfiTinvaZ-bridge, made by Mazda ; we sacrifice unto Apam Napi/, the swift-horsed, the high and shining lord, who has many wives ; and we sacrifice unto the water, made by Mazda and holy; we sacrifice unto the golden and tall Haoma; we sacrifice unto the enlivening Haoma, who makes the world grow ; we sacrifice unto Haoma, who keeps death far away ; we sacrifice unto the pious and good Blessing ; we sacrifice unto the awful, powerful, cursing thought of the wise, a God ; we sacrifice unto all the holy Gods of the heavenly world ; we sacrifice unto all the holy Gods of the material world.
I praise, I invoke, I meditate upon, and we sacrifice unto the good, strong, beneficent Fravashis of the holy ones \ » Cf. Yasna XXVI, i.
ORMAZD YAyr. 21 I, ORMAZD YA^r.

The Onnazd Yajt, properly so called, ends with § 23. The rest of the Ya^t, from § 24 to the end, is wanting in several manuscripts, and is supposed by the Parsis to be a fragment of the Bahman YarL The Onnazd Yart is exclusively devoted to an enumeration of the names of Ahura and to a laudation of their virtues and efficacy: the recitation of these names is the best defence against all dangers.
§§ 1-6. The names of Ahura Mazda are the most powerful part of the Holy Word.
§§ 7-8. The twenty names of Ahura Mazda are enumerated $§ 9-11. Efficacy of these names.
S§ 12-15. Another list of names.
§§ 16-19. Efficacy of Ahura's names.
§§ 20-23. Sundry formulas of invocation.

As may be seen from this summary, the subject has been treated twice over, first in §§ i-ii, and then in §§ 12-19; yet it does not appear that this Yart was formed out of two independent treatises, and it is more likely that the vague and indefinite enumeration in S§ 12-15, which interrupts so clumsily the train of ideas, is due either to an interpolation or simply to the literary deficiency of the writer himself.

The Onnazd Yart is recited every day at the H&van G&h, after the morning prayer (Anquetil, Zend-Avesta, II, 143) : it is well also to recite it when going to sleep and when changing one's residence (§ 17).

Speculations on the mystical powers of God's names have always been common among Orientals. The number of these names went on increasing: Dast&r Ndshtrvin wrote on the loi names of God; Dastfir Marzb&n on his 125 names. With the Musulmans, Allah had looi names. On the names of God among the Jews, see Zeitschrift der Deutschen Morgenlandischen Gesellschaft, XXXV, pp. 162, 532.

We have three native translations of this Yart ; one in Pahlavi (East India Office, XII, 39, and St. Petersburg, XCIX, 39 ; edited by Carl Salemann), one in Persian (East India Office, XXII, 43), and one in Sanskrit (Paris, fonds Burnouf, V, 66) ; the last two edited in our Etudes Iraniennes, II, 255).
22 YAOTS AND stRdZAHS.

The second part of the Yart, the so-called Bahman Yait fragment, is in a state of the utmost corruption. It is difficult to trace any connection in the ideas, yet §§ 28, 29, 30 seem to point rather clearly to the final struggle between Ormazd and Ahriman and to the annihilation of the Da6vas, and, thereby, some connection is established between this fragment and the Pahlavi Bahman YaxtS which deals with the same subject. If that correspondence be real, § 26 might refer to the beginning of the Fahlavi Bahman Yaxt, in which Zarathurtra is shown by Ahura the times to come and the end of the world.
Of this fragment we have only a bad Pahlavi translation in the St. Petersburg manuscript mentioned above.
o'. May Ahura Mazda be rejoiced! May Angra Mainyn be destroyed ! by those who do truly what is the foremost wish (of God»).
I praise well-thought, well-spoken, and well-done thoughts, words, and deeds. I embrace all good thoughts, good words, and good deeds ; I reject all evil thoughts, evil words, and evil deeds.

I give sacrifice and prayer unto you, O Amesha-Spentas I even with the fulness of my thoughts, of my words, of my deeds, and of my heart : I give unto you even my own life ^ I recite the * Praise of Holiness':'

'Ashem Vohft : Holiness is the best of all good. Well is it for it, well is it for that holiness which is perfection of holiness V I confess myself a worshipper of Mazda, a follower of Zarathurtra, one who hates the Da6vas and obeys the laws of Ahura*;
zh: 我們祭祀阿希‧凡古希（Ashi Vanguhi），光明的、高尚的、強大的、身材高挑的、仁慈的；我們祭祀由馬茲達所造的榮耀；我們祭祀由馬茲達所造的福祉。我們祭祀帕雷迪（Pāreḍi），擁有光輝戰車者；我們祭祀由馬茲達所造的阿里亞人的榮耀；我們祭祀由馬茲達所造的恐怖的王者榮耀；我們祭祀由馬茲達所造的那無法被強行奪取的恐怖榮耀；我們祭祀由馬茲達所造的查拉圖斯特拉的榮耀。
26. 阿提德（A^tid）
我們祭祀阿日茲特（Arjt&Z），彼使世界生長；我們祭祀馬茲達所造之烏希‧達瑞納山（Mount Ushi-darena），聖福之神。
27. 阿斯敏(Asmin)。
我們祭祀閃耀的諸天；我們祭祀光明、圓滿、至福的聖者淨土。
28. 則彌得（Zemydd）。
我們祭祀大地，一位有益的神祇；我們祭祀這些地方，祭祀這些田野；我們祭祀烏希達雷納山 (Mount Ushi-darena)，由摩茲達所造，一位聖潔幸福的神祇；我們祭祀所有的山嶺，它們是聖潔幸福的座席、圓滿幸福的座席，由摩茲達所造，聖潔者及聖潔的主宰；我們祭祀畏懼的王者榮光，由摩茲達所造；我們祭祀無法被強行奪取的畏懼榮光，由摩茲達所造。
29. 馬赫拉斯潘德
我們祭祀光榮崇高的密特拉‧斯畢塔（Mithra Speṭa）；我們祭祀反對諸惡魔（Daevas）的法則；我們祭祀瑣羅亞斯德的法則；我們祭祀長久傳統的教誨；我們祭祀馬茲達崇拜者的善法；我們祭祀對密特拉‧斯畢塔（Mithra Speṭa）的虔誠奉獻；我們祭祀堅守馬茲達崇拜者之法則的智慧；我們祭祀《雅斯特與斯羅查》（Yasts and Sroṭahs）。
我們祭祀彌特拉‧斯培塔（Mithra Spenta）的知識；我們祭祀由瑪茲達所造的天界「智慧」；我們祭祀通過耳聞而獲得、由瑪茲達所造的智慧。

30. 吾等祭祀永恆與至尊光明的空間；吾等祭祀光輝的迦洛納（Garōnmāna）；吾等祭祀至尊永恆福祉之地；吾等祭祀由馬茲達所造之金橋（jiiinvazl-bridge）；吾等祭祀阿帕姆‧那庇（Apam Napi），速駿之馬的主人、高遠光耀之尊，具有眾多妻妾者；吾等祭祀由馬茲達所造與聖聖之水；吾等祭祀金色與高大的贺嗎（Haoma）；吾等祭祀賦予生機之贺嗎，使世界得以生長者；吾等祭祀將死亡遠遠推離之贺嗎；吾等祭祀虔誠而善良之福祉；吾等祭祀可畏、強大、發出詛咒之思想，聖者之思；吾等祭祀天界一切聖聖的神；吾等祭祀物質世界一切聖聖的神。
我讚頌、祈求、冥想於善良、強大、慈惠的聖者之靈魂（Fravashi），並以此獻祭。\ » 參見《雅斯那經》第二十六篇第一節。

我注意到您提供的英文原文非常簡短且似乎不完整： ``` ORMAZD YAyr. 21 I, ORMAZD YA^r. ``` 這段文字看起來像是文獻索引或片段記錄，而非完整的段落。請您確認： 1. 這是否為完整的原文？ 2. 是否有更多的上下文或完整段落？ 3. 「ORMAZD」（瑣羅亞斯德教的智慧之神）後面的「YAyr」和「YA^r」是否為特定的文獻編碼？ 若您能提供更完整的英文原文，我將能為您提供準確的繁體中文翻譯。
《歐馬茲德雅什特》(Onnazd Yajt)，嚴格來說，在第23節結束。雅什特的其餘部分，從第24節到末尾，在若干手稿中付之闕如，且瑣羅亞斯德教信徒認為乃是《巴赫曼雅什特》(Bahman Yarl)的殘篇。《歐馬茲德雅什特》專門用於列舉阿胡拉(Ahura)的各種名號，並頌讚其德行與效驗；誦唸這些名號乃是抵禦一切危厄的最佳防護。
§ 1-6. 阿胡拉‧馬茲達（Ahura Mazda）的諸聖名乃聖言中最強有力之部分。
§ § 7-8. 阿胡拉‧馬茲達的二十個名號被一一列舉 § 9-11. 這些名號的功效
§ 16-19. 阿胡拉聖名的效能
§ 20-23. 各種祈禱文
從此摘要可以看出，該主題已被處理過兩次，首先在§§ i-ii 中，然後在§§ 12-19 中；然而並未顯示這份 Yart 是由兩份獨立的論文編製而成，更可能的是§§ 12-15 中含糊且不確定的列舉——它如此生硬地中斷了思想的進行——要麼是源於插補，要麼就是源於作者本身的文學水平不足。
《歐瑪茲德讚文》(Onnazd Yart) 每日在晨禱後於火廟儀式 (H&van G&h) 中誦讀（安凱蒂爾，《智慧經‧阿維斯陀》，第二卷，第143頁）：入睡時及遷居時誦讀此文亦為善舉（第17節）。

關於上帝之名的神祕力量的思辨，向來在東方民族中盛行。這些聖名的數目不斷增加：達斯圖爾‧納什特瓦因（Dast&r Ndshtrvin）曾著述論述上帝的九十九個聖名；達斯圖爾‧馬爾茲班（Dastfir Marzb&n）則論述其一百二十五個聖名。在穆斯林傳統中，真主（Allah）有一百個聖名。關於猶太人中的上帝之名，詳見《德國東方學會期刊》（Zeitschrift der Deutschen Morgenlandischen Gesellschaft），第三十五卷，第一百六十二及五百三十二頁。
我們擁有此《雅斯那》(Yart) 的三份本地譯本：一份為帕拉維文譯本（東印度辦事處，XII, 39，及聖彼得堡，XCIX, 39；由卡爾‧薩勒曼〔Carl Salemann〕編訂），一份為波斯文譯本（東印度辦事處，XXII, 43），一份為梵文譯本（巴黎，布爾努夫基金會，V, 66）；後兩份編入我們的《伊朗研究》(Etudes Iraniennes)，第二卷，第 255 頁。
二十二 瑜伽行者與蘇菲行者

《雅斯特》的第二部分，即所謂的《巴赫曼‧雅斯特》（Bahman Yait）殘篇，文本狀況極為混亂。難以追蹤其中思想的任何連貫性，然而第28、29、30節似乎頗為清晰地指向歐瑪茲德（Ormazd）與阿赫里曼（Ahriman）之間的最終鬥爭，以及達瓦斯（Da6vas）的消滅，由此在此殘篇與帕赫拉維《巴赫曼‧雅克特》（Pahlavi Bahman YaxtS）之間建立了某種連繫——後者亦涉及同一主題。若此對應關係確實存在，第26節可能指涉帕赫拉維《巴赫曼‧雅克特》的開篇，其中瑣羅亞斯德（Zarathurtra）由阿胡拉（Ahura）被啟示未來的時代與世界的終結。
關於此殘卷，我們僅存一份不佳的波斯語（Pahlavi）譯本，出自上述聖彼得堡手稿。
啊！願阿胡拉‧瑪茲達（Ahura Mazda）欣喜！願安格拉‧邁紐（Angra Mainyu）被摧毀！由那些真正行做上帝至高之願者為之。
我讚美周思而慮、周思而言、周思而行的思想、言語與行為。我擁抱一切善念、善言與善行；我排斥一切惡念、惡言與惡行。
我以我思念的圓滿、我言語的圓滿、我行為的圓滿，以及我心靈的圓滿，向你們奉獻祭祀與祈禱，阿梅沙-斯彭塔斯 (Amesha-Spentas)！我甚至將我自己的生命奉獻予你們。我誦讀《聖潔的讚美》(Praise of Holiness)。
『阿舍姆‧沃赫圖』：聖潔乃是一切善之中最美好者。對於聖潔本身而言福祉備至，對於那完美聖潔的聖潔而言福祉備至。 我承認自己是瑪茲達（Mazda）的崇拜者，是查拉圖斯特拉（Zarathurtra）的追隨者，是仇恨諸惡魔（Daêvas）且服從阿胡拉（Ahura）法律的人；
